structure.affirmative: Subject + will have + V3 — I will have finished. / She will have arrived.
structure.negative: Subject + won't have + V3 — I won't have finished. / She won't have arrived.
structure.question: Will + Subject + have + V3? — Will you have finished? / Will she have arrived?

usage.text: Action completed before a specific future moment
Duration that will be complete at a future point

faq.q: What's the difference between Future Perfect and Future Simple?
faq.a: Future Simple ('will finish') just states that something will happen. Future Perfect ('will have finished') stresses that it will be completed before a given future moment: 'By 6 pm I will have finished' — done before 6 pm, not at 6 pm.

faq.q: Does Future Perfect replace 'when' in time clauses?
faq.a: In a 'when/by the time' time clause itself, use Present Perfect, not Future Perfect: 'When she has arrived, we will start.' Future Perfect goes in the main clause: 'By the time she arrives, I will have cooked dinner.'
The third form of the verb. Regular: finish→finished. Irregular: go→gone, write→written, leave→left. Same form as in Present Perfect and Past Perfect.
